items.prompt: interprets and solves bounded tasks involving rational, radical, and exponential functions while preserving family-specific constraints
items.expected_evidence: solve bounded equations and comparisons while preserving domain restrictions and family meaning
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner solves the task accurately while respecting the structural constraints of the chosen function family.

items.prompt: explains why asymptotes, domain cutoffs, or multiplicative growth emerge from rational, radical, or exponential structure
items.expected_evidence: explain why asymptotes, domain cutoffs, or multiplicative growth arise from the symbolic structure
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner explains advanced-function behavior as a consequence of structure rather than as memorized graph facts.

items.prompt: Diagnose a peer error aligned to extraneous_or_excluded_solution_acceptance.
items.expected_evidence: identify the family and key structural constraints of bounded rational, radical, and exponential functions
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner identifies where a candidate solution violates a family-specific constraint or was not checked against the original structure.

items.prompt: interprets a less-familiar advanced-function situation and selects the representation or family features that reveal the needed behavior
items.expected_evidence: compare representations to justify function-family-specific behavior in algebraic and graphical form
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner preserves coherent family meaning across representation and context.
